title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in responsive progressively enhanced performance budget and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.
Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Mobile First Shell
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 4188 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.

Inspect the Mobile First Shell trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Mobile First Shell.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency. For Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Mobile First Shell, keep Mobile First Shell connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: The Mobile First Shell record says responsive progressively enhanced performance budget looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Mobile First Shell.

For inspect the failure record, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Mobile First Shell for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.
The record names the assumption, applies Mobile First Shell, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-performance-evidence-123106
# Competency: ssg-performance-evidence.
# Case 1444: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_performance_evidence_123106(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_123106 = "123106-601321-1444"
    assert evidence_variant_123106
    evidence_axes_123106 = (
        "reader123106",
        "source1444",
        "parse601321",
        "route1231061444",
        "artifact1444123106",
        "recovery6013211444",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_123106)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
